# Primer

> Self-hosted leadership performance management platform, distributed as a perpetual source-code license. Customers receive a zip, run it on their own PostgreSQL + Node infrastructure (or via Docker Compose), and modify the source freely. No telemetry, no license server, no SaaS dependency at runtime.

This file is a structured map for AI assistants. Read `README.md` for installation and `CLAUDE.md` for codebase orientation. Both are at the project root.

## Stack
- TypeScript + SvelteKit 2 (Svelte 5 runes mode)
- `@sveltejs/adapter-node` (build artefact is a vanilla Node server)
- PostgreSQL 15+ via `postgres` (postgres-js, no ORM)
- Tailwind CSS 4 (theme tokens in `src/routes/layout.css` `@theme` block)
- Application-layer scrypt auth (`src/lib/server/auth/index.ts`); intended to be replaced with the customer's SSO

## Documentation
- [README.md](./README.md): Customer quickstart — installation (Option A direct install, Option B Docker Compose), configuration, customization, troubleshooting, air-gap procedure
- [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md): Orientation document for AI assistants and developers — project layout, conventions, non-negotiable rules (i18n, DB access, no telemetry, no RLS)
- [.env.example](./.env.example): Annotated configuration reference; copy to `.env` and edit
- [docs/architecture.md](./docs/architecture.md): Stack, request lifecycle, data model, integration seams (DB, auth, email, reverse proxy, monitoring, logs) for Option C planners
- [docs/data-flow.md](./docs/data-flow.md): What data is stored, where, and what (does not) leave the server — for compliance / privacy review
- [docs/ai-runbook.md](./docs/ai-runbook.md): Copy-paste prompts for running customization, integration, and diagnostic tasks through an AI coding assistant
- [docs/sbom.csv](./docs/sbom.csv): Software Bill of Materials, generated from `package-lock.json` (regenerate with `npm run sbom`)
- [docs/CHANGELOG.md](./docs/CHANGELOG.md): Version history (Keep a Changelog + SemVer); read before applying an update

## Source code
- [src/routes/auth/](./src/routes/auth/): Login / register / password reset / email verification (placeholder flow; expected to be replaced for SSO/OIDC/SAML deployments)
- [src/routes/app/](./src/routes/app/): The product surface — goals, peers, hierarchy, leaders, reports, team, settings, inquiries, admin
- [src/routes/api/health/](./src/routes/api/health/): Public liveness probe consumed by Docker Compose and external monitoring
- [src/lib/server/db.ts](./src/lib/server/db.ts): The single postgres-js client; `sql` tagged template plus `one`, `maybeOne`, `many` helpers. All server-side DB access goes through this file.
- [src/lib/server/auth/index.ts](./src/lib/server/auth/index.ts): Scrypt password hashing, session cookie management, email-verification and password-reset tokens
- [src/lib/server/permissions.ts](./src/lib/server/permissions.ts): The role × hierarchy authorization matrix; canonical entry points are `verifyManagementAccess` and `verifySnapshotAdjustAccess`
- [src/lib/server/hierarchy.ts](./src/lib/server/hierarchy.ts): Org-tree traversal helpers (subtree node IDs, ancestor checks)
- [src/lib/server/action-context.ts](./src/lib/server/action-context.ts): Rebuilds the org/membership/userNode context inside form actions (since SvelteKit actions don't get `parent()`)
- [src/lib/i18n/](./src/lib/i18n/): One JSON file per supported language (en, zh, es, ar, fr, de, ja, pt, ko); flat dot-namespaced keys; missing non-English keys fall back to English with a console warning
- [src/lib/types/index.ts](./src/lib/types/index.ts): Domain types — `TierLevel`, `OrgRole`, `HierarchyNodeType`, `OrgGoal`, `MetricOrigin`, etc.
- [src/hooks.server.ts](./src/hooks.server.ts): `handleLocale` (resolves locale from cookie + Accept-Language) → `handleAuth` (validates session, populates `locals.user`). Route-level guards live in `src/routes/app/+layout.server.ts`, which redirects to `/auth/login` when `locals.user` is null.

## Database
- [migrations/](./migrations/): SQL schema, applied in lexical order by `npm run migrate`. Tracked in `schema_migrations` table. Plain PostgreSQL; no Supabase features, no RLS.
- [seeds/](./seeds/): Demo data SQL files, applied by `npm run seed`. Tracked in `schema_seeds` table. Includes the Meridian Construction demo organization (login: `demo@primer.company` / `demo2025`).
- [scripts/migrate.ts](./scripts/migrate.ts): Migration runner
- [scripts/seed.ts](./scripts/seed.ts): Seed runner; also sets scrypt-hashed demo passwords on Meridian users post-seed

## Deployment
- [Dockerfile](./Dockerfile): Multi-stage build — build stage installs deps + builds + prunes; runtime stage is `node:20-alpine` with non-root user
- [docker-compose.yml](./docker-compose.yml): Three services — `app` (built locally), `db` (postgres:15-alpine, persistent volume `db-data`), `proxy` (caddy:2-alpine, ports 80/443)
- [docker/entrypoint.sh](./docker/entrypoint.sh): Always runs migrations; runs seed only on first boot (gated by `PRIMER_SEED_ON_BOOT` and a marker file); `exec node build` for clean SIGTERM
- [caddy/Caddyfile](./caddy/Caddyfile): Local-default `:80` reverse proxy with security headers; comments explain the one-edit path to a real hostname with automatic Let's Encrypt

## Non-negotiable rules
- **i18n**: Never hardcode user-facing strings. Use `t('key.name')`. Add new keys to `en.json` first, then mirror to all 8 other locales.
- **DB**: Use parameterized queries via the postgres-js tagged template. Never concatenate user input into SQL. Never modify existing migration files — add new ones.
- **Permissions**: Application-layer only (`src/lib/server/permissions.ts`). No Postgres RLS, no `auth.uid()` in SQL.
- **Telemetry**: None. Primer makes zero outbound network calls at runtime. Do not add analytics, error reporters, or update checks.
- **Dependencies**: Add new ones reluctantly. The customer must trust and maintain every entry in `package.json`. Prefer copying ~50 lines over adding a 500-line library.
